title: Workload
description:
On-disk `workload.toml` manifest. Each variant matches one `ServiceComponent.kind` value; the `kind` field on the wire is the serde discriminator.

This is the **on-disk** envelope — distinct from [`WorkloadSpec`], the containerd wire format warden receives over RPC. A `kind = "container"` workload deserializes its remaining fields as a `WorkloadSpec`; other kinds carry their own per-reconciler payload shape.

oneOf.description: Static-site build that publishes an artifact directory to the service's `static` provider slot. Reconciled by the `mesofact-static` reconciler — does not deploy to warden.

build_mode.description: Where the build command runs. Default: `HostSide` (mesofact-dev on the host). Set to `InContainer` for cloud/HA where no host watcher is present and CI-fidelity build environments are required.

routes.description: Path (relative to the manifest) of the routes module the `mesofact-static` reconciler reads to enumerate routes.

ssr_runtime.description:
Optional SSR/SPA runtime companion container.

`None` → pure static site; Caddy (or equivalent CDN) serves all requests directly from the object store. This is the common case for dev-yah today.

`Some` → the workload spec describes a long-lived container that handles dynamic/SSR requests. Caddy routes static asset paths to the object store and all other paths to this container. The companion uses `RestartPolicy::Always`; the orchestrator (camp or warden) ensures it stays up alongside the Caddy edge.

oneOf.description: Containerd workload handed to warden over RPC. The inline fields are the full [`WorkloadSpec`] minus the `kind` discriminator.

annotations.description: Yah-specific metadata, conventionally prefixed `yah.*`. Opaque to warden beyond `yah.forge=true` which suppresses the Never-restart guard.

command.description: Override the image's `CMD`. `None` leaves the image default.

depends_on.description: Mesh idents that must reach `Ready` before this workload starts.

entrypoint.description: Override the image's `ENTRYPOINT`. `None` leaves the image default.

env.description: Environment variables. Values may be literals, secret refs, or mesh-address references resolved by warden at deploy time.

expose.description: Network exposure configuration — mesh, public, and operator channels are independent and can be set in any combination.

labels.description: OCI-style labels, passed through to the container. Opaque to warden.

name.description: DNS-friendly workload name, e.g. `"noisetable-api"`. Regex: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?$`, length ≤ 63.

replicas.description: Target replica count. `0` registers the workload without deploying it. Range: 0–100 (cluster-wide cap; operator can raise it).

resources.description: Hard resource caps enforced by containerd/cgroups.

restart_policy.description: What warden does when the container exits.

schema_version.description: Wire-format version; always `V1` today. Present at the top level so rolling clusters can detect and migrate across schema generations.

secrets.description: Secret mounts. Values never appear in the spec JSON — only references.

tier.description: Tier tag controlling admission control and mesh filtering.

user.description: User to run as, e.g. `"1000:1000"` or `"appuser"`.

oneOf.description: Data-pipeline job with declared I/O and a readiness policy. The orchestrator checks all `inputs` are reachable before each run and verifies `outputs` afterward. Generalises the OpenRouter JSON-cache refresher (`spawn_almanac_refresher`) to the full manifest form.

command.description: Shell command executed via `sh -c` from the workload directory.

inputs.description: Input targets that must be reachable before the command runs. Empty list → no precondition checks (degenerate case).

invalidates.description: Mesh identities of workloads to notify after a successful run. The orchestrator sends a cache-bust signal to each entry so downstream consumers can reload their data (e.g. mesofact-dev triggers a rebuild when the OpenRouter cache refreshes). Empty list → no downstream invalidation.

not_ready_policy.description: What to do when a precondition check fails. Default: `WaitWithTimeout { timeout: 5000ms }`.

outputs.description: Output targets verified after a successful run. Empty list → no post-run verification.

AlmanacTarget.description:
An observable endpoint the almanac scheduler probes to check readiness.

Used for both inputs (checked before the run) and outputs (verified after a successful run to confirm the job produced something reachable). The probe is intentionally lightweight — no S3 SigV4, no xlb-net discovery required; a simple TCP connect or HTTP GET is enough for the dev/sim tier.

oneOf.description: Issue an HTTP GET to `url`; ready when the server responds with `expect_status` (default: any 2xx).
Establish a TCP connection to `host:port`; ready when the connect succeeds. Used for non-HTTP services (e.g. MinIO API on port 9000) and as a lighter probe when an HTTP endpoint isn't stable yet.

initial_ms.description: Initial delay before the first restart, in milliseconds.

max_ms.description: Maximum delay between retries, in milliseconds.

multiplier.description: Backoff multiplier applied to each successive delay.

BuildConfig.description: Build step that produces the static artifact published by a `mesofact-static` workload.

command.description: Shell command run from the manifest's directory, e.g. `"bun run build"`.

out_dir.description: Output directory (relative to the manifest) the reconciler uploads.

BuildMode.description:
Where the build command runs for a `mesofact-static` workload.

The two-role split encodes the F7 design decision: build/publish is a **transient job** (runs once, exits, GC'd); SSR/SPA serving is a separate **long-lived companion container** (optional, only for dynamic pages). A single merged "mesofact container" is the trap — in cloud, CI builds the artifact, R2+CDN serve it, and a distinct worker handles any SSR.

Default: `HostSide` — mesofact-dev runs the build on the host and publishes to the tier's object store. No container overhead; compatible with dev and sim tiers.

oneOf.description: Build command runs on the host (mesofact-dev watcher). The watcher publishes the output to the tier's object store (DistPointer for dev, MinIO for sim). Compatible with all tiers; zero container overhead.

Build runs inside a transient container matching the CI image. Higher fidelity (environment matches CI exactly); costs image pull + container cold-start. Required for cloud/HA where no mesofact-dev watcher is running on the host.

image.description: Container image that runs the build (e.g. `"ghcr.io/org/app-build:v1.2"`). Must have the build toolchain installed. The container is started with the workspace root bind-mounted, runs `build.command`, uploads `build.out_dir` to the object store, then exits.

Cadence.description: When the almanac scheduler triggers a run.

oneOf.description: Run once at first opportunity, then never again.
Run repeatedly with a fixed interval between the end of one run and the start of the next. Equivalent to `sleep N && run` in a loop.

interval.description: Minimum time between consecutive run completions.

oneOf.description: Run on a UTC cron schedule (standard 5-field expression, e.g. `"0 */6 * * *"` for every 6 hours). The scheduler evaluates the expression relative to UTC midnight.

EnvValue.description: Value source for an environment variable.

oneOf.description: Static string baked into the spec.
Resolved from a warden secret at deploy time; the secret value never appears in the spec JSON.
Resolved from another workload's mesh address at deploy time by warden. Lets workloads reference each other symbolically without IP pinning.

EnvVar.description: A single environment variable injected into the container.

ExposeSpec.description: Network exposure configuration. The three channels are independent; any combination is valid.

mesh.description: Mesh-internal exposure. Required; every workload must have a mesh identity even if no other workload currently reaches it.

operator.description: Operator-facing exposure via a Tailscale ACL tag. `None` means the workload is not operator-reachable via Tailscale.

public.description: Public internet exposure via a Cloudflare tunnel route. `None` means the workload is not internet-reachable.

HealthProbe.description: Mechanism used to check container health.

oneOf.description: HTTP GET to `path` on `port`. A 2xx (or `expect_status` if set) response counts as healthy.
Run `argv` inside the container; exit-0 counts as healthy.
TCP connection to `port`; a successful connect counts as healthy.

failure_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before the container is marked `Unhealthy`.

initial_delay.description: Time to wait after container start before the first probe. Shape validation warns (not errors) if this is less than `stop_policy.grace_period * 2`.

probe.description: The probe executed to determine container health.

timeout.description: Per-probe timeout; a slow response counts as failure.

ImageRef.description: Container image reference identifying a specific image to pull.

digest.description: Content-addressed pinned identity, e.g. `"sha256:..."`. Takes precedence over `tag` for exact-image guarantees when present.

tag.description: Tag, e.g. `"v1.4.2"` or `"latest"`. Must be non-empty when `digest` is `None`; shape validation enforces this.

MeshExpose.description: Mesh-internal port exposure and peer access control.

allow_from.description: Which tiers may initiate connections to this workload on the mesh. An empty list means no peer restriction (warden default: allow all).

identity.description: DNS-segment mesh identity for this workload. Must be unique in the cluster. Regex: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?$`, length ≤ 63.

ports.description: Container-side ports this workload listens on. Other workloads reach it at `<identity>:<port>` on the mesh.

MeshIdent.description: DNS-segment identity for a workload on the cluster mesh, e.g. `"noisetable-api.pdx"`. Regex constraint: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?$`, length ≤ 63. Enforced in shape validation (R090-F2).

MeshLookup.description: Which aspect of a mesh peer's address to inject.

oneOf.description: Full URL, e.g. `"http://noisetable-db.pdx:5432"`.
Hostname only, e.g. `"noisetable-db.pdx"`.
Port only, e.g. `"5432"`.

Millis.description:
Duration expressed as an integer millisecond count.

Used for healthcheck intervals, timeouts, delays, and stop grace periods. Chosen over `std::time::Duration` to keep serde support dependency-free.

NotReadyPolicy.description:
What the almanac scheduler does when a precondition check fails.

The F9 design decision: `WaitWithTimeout` is the default. Fail-fast is too brittle for the sim tier (containers may still be cold-starting); requeue-with-no-ceiling can block the scheduler indefinitely. The recommended timeout for sim is the container spinup budget (~5 s cold, ~1 s warm): set `timeout` to a few seconds, then let the retry cadence handle transient glitches.

oneOf.description: Wait up to `timeout` for all preconditions to pass before aborting the run. The run is skipped (not rescheduled); the next cadence tick will retry. Suitable when targets occasionally lag at startup.

timeout.description: How long to wait for each precondition to become reachable. The scheduler polls with a short sleep between attempts.

oneOf.description: Abort immediately if any precondition check fails. Suitable for integration-test harnesses where a missing dependency is always a hard error.
Requeue with exponential backoff up to `max_attempts` times. After exhaustion the run is marked failed. Suitable for cloud/HA where transient dependency outages are expected.

backoff.description: Initial backoff between attempts, in milliseconds.

max_attempts.description: Maximum number of requeue attempts before the run is marked failed.

OperatorExpose.description: Operator-facing exposure via a Tailscale ACL tag.

port.description: Container-side port to expose to Tailscale-authorized operators.

tailscale_tag.description: Tailscale ACL tag granting access, e.g. `"tag:noisetable-ops"`. Semantic validation checks that this tag exists in the cluster's Tailscale ACL.

PublicExpose.description: Public internet exposure via a Cloudflare tunnel route.

hostname.description: Public hostname to route, e.g. `"api.noisetable.io"`. Semantic validation checks that this hostname is owned by a configured CF zone.

port.description: Container-side port to route traffic to. Shape validation requires this port to appear in `expose.mesh.ports`.

tls.description: TLS configuration for the public endpoint.

PublicTls.description: TLS mode for a public endpoint.

oneOf.description: Cloudflare manages the TLS certificate (default; requires a proxied DNS record in the configured zone).
User-supplied certificate referenced by name in the warden secret store.

ResourceLimits.description: Hard resource caps enforced by containerd/cgroups at runtime.

ephemeral_storage_mb.description: Cap on the writable layer + tmpfs footprint, in MiB.

memory_mb.description: Maximum RAM the container may allocate, in MiB. The container is OOM- killed if it exceeds this.

RestartPolicy.description: What warden does when the container exits.

oneOf.description: Restart unconditionally on any exit.
Restart on non-zero exit, up to `max_attempts` times with exponential backoff. After exhaustion, the workload is marked `Failed`.

Do not restart. The container runs once and exits.

**Forge convention.** Forge runs (R094) synthesize a `WorkloadSpec` using [`WorkloadSpec::for_forge`] which sets all the conventional fields together:

- `restart_policy = Never` - `expose.public = None`, `expose.operator = None` - `expose.mesh.identity = "forge.<forge_id>"` — distinguishable from persistent mirror identities at the mesh layer - `tier = "infra"` (or the forge-spec's effective tier) - `annotations["yah.forge"] = "true"` — suppresses the shape warning

Using `Never` on a persistent mirror (not a forge run) means the mirror stays dead after any exit — a likely misconfiguration. Shape validation emits a soft warning unless `annotations["yah.forge"] == "true"` is present. See R094 forge.

SchemaVersion.description:
Wire-format schema version envelope.

Single variant today. When a breaking field rename or removal requires a migration path, a new variant is added. The `schema_version` field on `WorkloadSpec` uses this as a tag so rolling clusters can decode multiple versions simultaneously. See arch doc §Evolution for the versioning rules.

SecretMount.description:
A secret value mounted into the container as an env var or file.

The secret value never appears in the spec JSON — only the reference. Warden audits secret access per workload from these references.

source.description: Where warden reads the secret value from.

target.description: How the secret is surfaced inside the container.

SecretRef.description: Where warden resolves the secret value from.

oneOf.description: Per-machine warden secret store at `/var/lib/yah/warden/secrets/`.
Raft-replicated cluster secret spanning all machines (planned; not in V1 deployment). Sketch preserved for wire compatibility.

SecretTarget.description: How the secret is surfaced inside the container.

oneOf.description: Injected as an environment variable. Value never appears in spec JSON. Prefer `File` — env vars leak through subprocess env and log dumps.
Mounted as a file inside the container at `path` with `mode` (octal).

StopPolicy.description: Graceful shutdown configuration for warden's stop sequence.

grace_period.description: Time warden waits after sending `signal` before issuing SIGKILL.

signal.description: Signal number sent first, e.g. `15` (SIGTERM) or `2` (SIGINT).

TierTag.description: Tier classification that governs admission control and mesh `allow_from` filtering. Known values: `"public"`, `"tenant"`, `"private"`, `"infra"`. Custom tiers are allowed per cluster; shape validation warns on unknowns rather than rejecting them (R090-F2).

VolumeMount.description: A volume mount inside the container.

read_only.description: Whether the container sees the volume as read-only.

VolumeSource.description: Backing source for a volume mount.

oneOf.description: Warden-managed named volume; created on first use.
Operator-managed host path. Warden rejects bind mounts unless `WorkloadSpec.tier == "infra"`; shape validation enforces this.
In-memory tmpfs; discarded on container stop. `size_mb` caps space consumed by the writable layer.

WorkloadSpec.description:
Complete typed description of a containerd workload handed to warden over RPC. This is also the payload of the `kind = "container"` variant of [`Workload`] on disk.

Warden never accepts compose YAML on its RPC surface — agents, the desktop, and operator CLIs all hand warden `WorkloadSpec` values. See the arch doc for the validation layers and evolution rules.

annotations.description: Yah-specific metadata, conventionally prefixed `yah.*`. Opaque to warden beyond `yah.forge=true` which suppresses the Never-restart guard.

command.description: Override the image's `CMD`. `None` leaves the image default.

depends_on.description: Mesh idents that must reach `Ready` before this workload starts.

entrypoint.description: Override the image's `ENTRYPOINT`. `None` leaves the image default.

env.description: Environment variables. Values may be literals, secret refs, or mesh-address references resolved by warden at deploy time.

expose.description: Network exposure configuration — mesh, public, and operator channels are independent and can be set in any combination.

labels.description: OCI-style labels, passed through to the container. Opaque to warden.

name.description: DNS-friendly workload name, e.g. `"noisetable-api"`. Regex: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?$`, length ≤ 63.

replicas.description: Target replica count. `0` registers the workload without deploying it. Range: 0–100 (cluster-wide cap; operator can raise it).

resources.description: Hard resource caps enforced by containerd/cgroups.

restart_policy.description: What warden does when the container exits.

schema_version.description: Wire-format version; always `V1` today. Present at the top level so rolling clusters can detect and migrate across schema generations.

secrets.description: Secret mounts. Values never appear in the spec JSON — only references.

tier.description: Tier tag controlling admission control and mesh filtering.

user.description: User to run as, e.g. `"1000:1000"` or `"appuser"`.
